meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't collaborate (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't collaborate. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Master Rapid Collaboration for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with collaboration bottlenecks. Learn to deploy a professional SaaS starter kit using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster than ever.
content.answer_block: Collaboration bottlenecks kill freelance projects. By leveraging a pre-configured SaaS starter kit, you unify your architecture, standardize deployments, and automate environment synchronization. Utilizing Antigravity for structural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative logic implementation allows you to bypass manual setup, enabling seamless multi-developer workflows and instant production readiness.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and your codebase is a siloed mess. You’re trapped in the 'can’t collaborate' loop—where every pull request is a headache and environment drift renders your local setup a ghost town. The frustration of manual synchronization isn't just a productivity drain; it's a direct threat to your reputation as a high-velocity developer.

But what if you could treat your architecture as an immutable, shared asset? By shifting to a standardized SaaS starter kit, you transform your workflow from a chaotic solo grind into a streamlined, repeatable assembly line. This isn't just about code; it's about building a scalable foundation that turns complex collaboration into a push-button operation.

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Establish the foundational structure of your SaaS using Antigravity to ensure consistent dependency management and folder hierarchy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a modular SaaS architecture using Next.js 14, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase. Include directory structures for services, hooks, and shared UI components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Scaffold the project directory structure as defined in the Antigravity spec.'

steps.title: Authentication & Session Persistence
steps.description: Implement a shared authentication layer so that every team member experiences the exact same user state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Supabase Auth middleware with strict session headers and server-side redirects for protected routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement middleware.ts for session management and route protection'

steps.title: Standardizing API Contracts
steps.description: Use TypeScript interfaces to enforce strict API contracts, preventing the 'I don't know what this endpoint returns' issue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Zod schemas and TypeScript interfaces for all CRUD operations in the SaaS starter kit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate Zod schemas based on existing database tables and export to types/api.ts'

steps.title: CI/CD Environment Synchronization
steps.description: Automate the environment configuration so that local, staging, and production environments are identical.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that validates environment variables and runs linting/testing before any merge.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Create .github/workflows/ci.yml with caching and type checking'

steps.title: Collaborative Documentation Injection
steps.description: Embed live documentation directly into the codebase so the starter kit is self-documenting for any future collaborators.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a docs/CONTRIBUTING.md file and integrate a README generator that pulls metadata from the project config.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate CONTRIBUTING.md and link to the internal documentation architecture'

pro_tips: Always use a 'shared-types' package within your monorepo to ensure frontend and backend are never out of sync.
Implement 'feature flags' early to allow collaborators to push half-finished code to production without breaking the UI.
Use Claude Code's 'review' feature on every PR to enforce your team's specific coding standards automatically.
Treat your environment variables as code: maintain a template file that Antigravity populates during local setup.
Leverage Git hooks (Husky) to force local linting before any commit to prevent 'collaboration friction' caused by style mismatches.
content.next_steps: Deploy your new starter kit to Vercel, invite your first collaborator to the repo, and run a full integration test to ensure environment parity.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your tools and start shipping features that your clients actually love. Master the modern stack and reclaim your time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
